trade.summary: Thailand is Japan's second-largest ASEAN trading partner and hosts one of the largest concentrations of Japanese manufacturing investment globally. Japanese automakers (Toyota, Honda, Mitsubishi, Isuzu) treat Thailand as their primary Southeast Asian production hub. The two countries have a bilateral EPA and are both RCEP and CPTPP members.

diplomatic.summary: Japan-Thailand relations are among Japan's closest in Southeast Asia, with over 150 years of formal diplomatic ties (dating from 1887). Thailand is the only Southeast Asian country never colonized by a European power, and its independent diplomatic tradition aligns with Japan's non-interventionist approach. Both countries cooperate closely on ASEAN issues, infrastructure, and development. Thailand's constitutional monarchy finds common ground with Japan's own monarchical tradition.

military.summary: Growing defense relationship. Japan has provided patrol vessels to the Royal Thai Navy. Both countries participate in multilateral ASEAN-affiliated exercises. Thailand's position as an ASEAN central player makes it strategically relevant to Japan's regional security posture. Japan's increasing defense exports (patrol vessels, amphibious vehicles) find a receptive market in Thailand.

financial.summary: Japan is Thailand's largest source of FDI, with cumulative investment in the billions spanning automotive, electronics, retail, and financial services. The Eastern Economic Corridor (EEC) has attracted major new Japanese investment commitments. Japanese trading companies, banks (MUFG, SMBC), and manufacturers have extensive Thai operations. Toyota alone employs tens of thousands in Thailand.

energy.summary: Japan imports some LNG and petrochemicals from Thailand. Japanese energy companies have invested in Thai natural gas exploration and distribution. Growing cooperation on renewable energy and energy efficiency aligned with Thailand's carbon neutrality goals.

scientific.summary: Extensive scientific and educational cooperation. The Japan International Cooperation Agency (JICA) runs numerous development programs in Thailand. Japanese universities have partnerships with Thailand's top institutions. Research cooperation covers agriculture, disaster management, and environmental science.
